claim: Dark factories use Archon workflows to run deterministic, repeatable AI coding tasks in orchestrated sequence (triage → implement → validate)
actionable: Structure your AI coding system with separate orchestration layers: governance (mission/rules/context), workflow engine (Archon), and looping agents to handle autonomous code generation
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Use the holdout pattern to prevent agent sycophancy: separate implementation agents from validation agents with zero context carryover to eliminate bias
actionable: When implementing code review pipelines, use two distinct agents/prompts: one writes code, a second validates using only diffs and user journeys, never the development context
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Dark factories succeed by encoding governance in markdown files (mission.md, factory rules, claw.md) that are loaded into every workflow to maintain alignment
actionable: Create explicit governance documents that define scope, out-of-scope items, implementation rules, and quality gates; load these files into agent context for every task
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Claude Code supports routing to different LLM providers via environment variables, enabling cost-optimized dark factories using cheaper models like MiniMax M2.7
actionable: Configure Claude Code with environment variables to route requests to MiniMax, Open Router, or other providers; this allows running continuous dark factories without hitting Anthropic rate limits
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Split PRs into small, concise changes to maintain code review quality; avoid allowing agents to create thousands-of-lines PRs that become unreviewable
actionable: Enforce maximum PR size limits in your factory rules; configure automated checks to reject oversized pull requests before they reach validation
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Browser automation testing (end-to-end user journey validation) is more reliable for dark factories than unit tests alone, which agents can rewrite to fake success
actionable: Use browser automation tools to test actual user workflows instead of relying solely on unit tests; agents cannot easily mock user-level behavior
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Regression testing should run weekly on documented user journeys to detect system-level failures that agents might miss during incremental implementation
actionable: Maintain a living document of all user journeys; run a separate weekly regression workflow that tests every interaction and files issues for failures
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)

claim: Strong DM and StrongDM have implemented production dark factories shipping thousands of lines of code with zero human hand-coding, proving the pattern works at scale
actionable: Research StrongDM's published blog posts and open-sourced specs to study their dark factory implementation for reference architecture patterns
source_title: I'm Building an AI Dark Factory That Ships Its Own Code (Public Experiment)
